latest_context.original_requirement: Can you make a simple time app in react? Simple, typescript, show the time in three cities of the world from a public API

rounds.question: For the new React+TypeScript time app, what project name/path would you like (e.g., 'time-app' under projects/) and should it fetch from the public API directly in the client or include a backend proxy?
rounds.answer: fetch it from the front-end and time-app is ok for a name

final_plan.summary: Implement a client‑only React+TypeScript single‑page app under projects/time-app that fetches world time data from a public API and displays live clocks for three cities.

bootstrap_commands.purpose: Bootstrap a new React+TypeScript project using Vite in non‑interactive mode
Start the development server for local testing

target_files.details: Defines project name, dependencies (React, React DOM, TypeScript, Vite plugin), and scripts for dev, build, and preview.
TypeScript compiler configuration as generated by the Vite template; ensure strict mode is enabled.
Vite config for React with TypeScript; verify any path aliases if needed.
Component that accepts city name and timezone endpoint path, fetches current time, and displays it updating every second.
Import and render three WorldClock components for the selected cities with basic layout and styling.
Ensure the App component is mounted to the DOM and global CSS is imported if needed.
Ignore node_modules, dist, and build artifacts.
Update the page title and meta description to reflect the time-app.
Usage instructions including install, dev, build commands and API usage notes.

constraints: Must be a client‑only React+TS SPA; no backend proxy
Use public time API endpoints without custom CORS workarounds
Keep dependencies minimal (no Redux or heavy state libraries)
Use functional components and React Hooks only
Follow repository conventions: root at projects/time-app
validation: `npm run dev` starts successfully and app loads in browser
The app displays live‑updating times for three cities
No TypeScript compilation errors (`npm run build` succeeds)
`npm run lint` yields no linting errors
clarification_summary: Fetch time data directly from front‑end using public API (no backend proxy)
review_guidelines: Confirm structure aligns with company repo conventions (projects root)
Ensure new files meet code style and lint rules
Validate correct use of React Hooks (cleanup in useEffect)
Verify proper type definitions for API responses
Check error handling for fetch failures and loading states
Test build and dev scripts from CLI
discovery_hints: Check existing ESLint/Prettier config in the root
Inspect root tsconfig.json if present for shared settings
Review root README for SPA best practices

goals: Provide a simple React+TS SPA that shows current time in three world cities
Use a public time API for fetching time data from the front-end
acceptance_criteria: Project scaffolded under projects/time-app with correct structure
Application fetches and displays time for three pre-defined cities without errors
TypeScript, lint, and build pipelines succeed without errors
README includes clear setup and usage instructions

ambiguities: Exact three cities were not specified; default to New York, London, Tokyo or confirm with product
